patches.hypothesis: The method was not accounting for the BC era in the Gregorian calendar
The method was not taking into account the BC era of the GregorianCalendar
patches.original_code:
//----------------------------------------------------------------------- /** * Constructs a LocalDateTime from a java.util.Calendar * using exactly the same field values. *

* Each field is queried from the Calendar and assigned to the LocalDateTime. * This is useful if you have been using the Calendar as a local date, * ignoring the zone. *

* One advantage of this method is that this method is unaffected if the * version of the time zone data differs between the JDK and Joda-Time. * That is because the local field values are transferred, calculated using * the JDK time zone data and without using the Joda-Time time zone data. *

* This factory method ignores the type of the calendar and always * creates a LocalDateTime with ISO chronology. It is expected that you * will only pass in instances of GregorianCalendar however * this is not validated. * * @param calendar the Calendar to extract fields from, not null * @return the created local date-time, not null * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the calendar is null * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the date is invalid for the ISO chronology */ public static LocalDateTime fromCalendarFields(Calendar calendar) { if (calendar == null) { throw new IllegalArgumentException("The calendar must not be null"); } int yearOfEra = calendar.get(Calendar.YEAR); return new LocalDateTime( yearOfEra, calendar.get(Calendar.MONTH) + 1, calendar.get(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH), calendar.get(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY), calendar.get(Calendar.MINUTE), calendar.get(Calendar.SECOND), calendar.get(Calendar.MILLISECOND) ); }

patches.hypothesis: The method was not handling the year field correctly for dates before the year 0.
patches.changes:
- yearOfEra = calendar.get(Calendar.YEAR);
+ yearOfEra = calendar.get(Calendar.ERA) == GregorianCalendar.BC ? 1 - calendar.get(Calendar.YEAR) : calendar.get(Calendar.YEAR);
patches.diff:
- yearOfEra = calendar.get(Calendar.YEAR);
+ yearOfEra = calendar.get(Calendar.ERA) == GregorianCalendar.BC ? 1 - calendar.get(Calendar.YEAR) : calendar.get(Calendar.YEAR);
patches.original_code:
//----------------------------------------------------------------------- /** * Constructs a LocalDateTime from a java.util.Calendar * using exactly the same field values. *

* Each field is queried from the Calendar and assigned to the LocalDateTime. * This is useful if you have been using the Calendar as a local date, * ignoring the zone. *

* One advantage of this method is that this method is unaffected if the * version of the time zone data differs between the JDK and Joda-Time. * That is because the local field values are transferred, calculated using * the JDK time zone data and without using the Joda-Time time zone data. *

* This factory method ignores the type of the calendar and always * creates a LocalDateTime with ISO chronology. It is expected that you * will only pass in instances of GregorianCalendar however * this is not validated. * * @param calendar the Calendar to extract fields from, not null * @return the created local date-time, not null * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the calendar is null * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the date is invalid for the ISO chronology */ public static LocalDateTime fromCalendarFields(Calendar calendar) { if (calendar == null) { throw new IllegalArgumentException("The calendar must not be null"); } int yearOfEra = calendar.get(Calendar.YEAR); return new LocalDateTime( yearOfEra, calendar.get(Calendar.MONTH) + 1, calendar.get(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH), calendar.get(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY), calendar.get(Calendar.MINUTE), calendar.get(Calendar.SECOND), calendar.get(Calendar.MILLISECOND) ); }

patches.hypothesis: The method was incorrectly using the year of era field from the calendar, rather than the actual year, which caused issues when dealing with dates before year 0.
patches.changes:
- yearOfEra = calendar.get(Calendar.YEAR);
+ int year = calendar.get(Calendar.ERA) == GregorianCalendar.AD ? calendar.get(Calendar.YEAR) : 1 - calendar.get(Calendar.YEAR);
patches.diff:
- yearOfEra = calendar.get(Calendar.YEAR);
+ int year = calendar.get(Calendar.ERA) == GregorianCalendar.AD ? calendar.get(Calendar.YEAR) : 1 - calendar.get(Calendar.YEAR);
patches.original_code:
//----------------------------------------------------------------------- /** * Constructs a LocalDateTime from a java.util.Calendar * using exactly the same field values. *

* Each field is queried from the Calendar and assigned to the LocalDateTime. * This is useful if you have been using the Calendar as a local date, * ignoring the zone. *

* One advantage of this method is that this method is unaffected if the * version of the time zone data differs between the JDK and Joda-Time. * That is because the local field values are transferred, calculated using * the JDK time zone data and without using the Joda-Time time zone data. *

* This factory method ignores the type of the calendar and always * creates a LocalDateTime with ISO chronology. It is expected that you * will only pass in instances of GregorianCalendar however * this is not validated. * * @param calendar the Calendar to extract fields from, not null * @return the created local date-time, not null * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the calendar is null * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the date is invalid for the ISO chronology */ public static LocalDateTime fromCalendarFields(Calendar calendar) { if (calendar == null) { throw new IllegalArgumentException("The calendar must not be null"); } int yearOfEra = calendar.get(Calendar.YEAR); return new LocalDateTime( yearOfEra, calendar.get(Calendar.MONTH) + 1, calendar.get(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH), calendar.get(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY), calendar.get(Calendar.MINUTE), calendar.get(Calendar.SECOND), calendar.get(Calendar.MILLISECOND) ); }

patches.hypothesis: The method was not properly handling the BC era in the calendar.
The bug is caused by the method not properly handling the case where the input calendar is in the BC era. The method was only handling the case where the input calendar is in the AD era.
patches.changes: The method now checks if the input calendar is in the BC era and adjusts the year accordingly before creating the LocalDate object.
patches.diff: The method now checks if the input calendar is in the BC era and adjusts the year accordingly before creating the LocalDate object.

patches.hypothesis: The method was incorrectly using the year of era instead of the year, causing the date to be off by one year for dates before year 0.
The year field was not being calculated correctly when the year was before year zero.
